summary: Added a new executable helper script, `tools/link.py`, for switching a decompiled C source file into the build in place of its corresponding assembly file. The tool edits `obj_files.mk` to replace the `asm/.../*.s` entry with `src/.../*.c`, updates `configure.py` by changing matching status from `NonMatching` to `Matching` for lines containing the source path, and deletes the linked assembly file.

key_files.change: New Python CLI tool that links a `src/.../*.c` file into the build by editing build metadata and removing the old asm file.

what_changed: Created `tools/link.py` as a new executable Python 3 script.
Added a `link(path: Path)` function that resolves the provided path relative to the repository `src` directory.
Derived corresponding source and assembly paths from the input path, including `src/.../*.c` and `asm/.../*.s` forms.
Added a generic `edit_file` helper that reads a file line-by-line, maps an edit operation across lines, drops `None` results, appends a final blank line, and writes the file back.
Added `edit_mk` logic for `obj_files.mk`: remove a line if it already contains the source path, otherwise replace the assembly path with the source path.
Added `edit_cfg` logic for `configure.py`: on lines containing the relative path, replace whole-word `NonMatching` with `Matching` using a regex.
After editing metadata, the script unlinks the corresponding asm file.
Added an argparse CLI with usage `link.py C_FILE` and description `replaces an asm file with a C file in the build`.
smart_moves: Used `Path(__file__).parents[1]` to anchor operations at the repository root instead of depending on the caller's current working directory.
Resolved the input path relative to `root / "src"`, allowing the script to consistently derive both `src` and `asm` path forms.
Used `Path.as_posix()` for stable slash-separated paths in build files.
Used `with_suffix` to derive `.s` and `.c` paths rather than manually slicing filenames.
Used a word-boundary regex, `\bNonMatching\b`, so status updates do not accidentally rewrite larger identifiers containing that string.
Kept the CLI minimal and task-focused: a single positional `C_FILE` argument performs the full link operation.
decomp_lessons: When a C translation unit is ready to replace its assembly counterpart, the project build metadata usually needs coordinated edits: object list replacement, configure matching-status update, and removal of the obsolete asm file.
A small linking helper can reduce repetitive manual errors during decomp work by deriving `asm/.../*.s` from `src/.../*.c` automatically.
For repository-modifying scripts, derive paths from the script location and repository layout rather than assuming the current directory.
When updating matching status in configuration files, constrain replacements to exact status tokens such as `NonMatching` to `Matching`.
naming_conventions: `tools/link.py` uses the verb `link` for the operation of replacing an asm build entry with a C source build entry.
CLI metavar is `C_FILE`, and help text documents expected input as `src/.../foo.c`.
The script uses local names `mk_path`, `cfg_path`, `src_str`, and `asm_str` to distinguish build files and derived string path forms.
Matching status spelling follows existing project convention: `NonMatching` and `Matching`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Convert a source path under `src` into the corresponding asm path under `asm` and with `.s` suffix.
Replace the asm path string in `obj_files.mk` with the source path string.
If `obj_files.mk` already contains the source path on a line, drop that line; the diff does not explain the intended duplicate-handling case beyond the code behavior.
Update `configure.py` lines containing the relative source path by changing `NonMatching` to `Matching`.
Delete the corresponding assembly file after the metadata changes are written.
follow_up_queries: Find later changes to tools/link.py for bug fixes or behavior changes.
Search for PRs that used link.py or changed obj_files.mk and configure.py together.
Check whether later tooling added an inverse unlink/unmatch operation.
Look for review or issue discussion about duplicate source entries in obj_files.mk.
